summary: Possible typosquat of keccak256 without malicious payload
details: Package name `kecak256` is a single-character deletion of the popular `keccak256` package. The shipped code is a small functional re-implementation that requires the `keccak` dependency and exports a hashing function matching the target package's API (kecak256.js calls `keccak('keccak256').update(value).digest()`). There are no install lifecycle scripts (`scripts` is absent from package.json), no network I/O, no filesystem or credential access, no obfuscation, and no dynamic code loading. Name confusion risk exists — an installer mistyping the legitimate package name would silently get this implementation — but the code itself does not exfiltrate data, run attacker-controlled payloads, or otherwise harm the installer. Routing to human review so a reviewer can confirm the name-confusion judgement and decide whether a public advisory is warranted.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
